root_cause.text: The `IssuerCheckConfiguration` was incorrectly conditioned on the `sso.connector.cloud.available` property, which prevented the issuer validation logic from being loaded when a resource server was not explicitly bound to an SSO service.
root_cause.evidence: The patch modifies `IssuerCheckConfiguration.java` to revert `@ConditionalOnProperty({"sso.connector.cloud.available"})` back to `@ConditionalOnProperty({"ssoServiceUrl", "security.oauth2.resource.jwk.key-set-uri"})`.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The `IssuerCheckConfiguration` was incorrectly conditioned on the `sso.connector.cloud.available` property, which prevented the issuer validation logic from being loaded when a resource server was not explicitly bound to an SSO service.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch modifies `IssuerCheckConfiguration.java` to revert `@ConditionalOnProperty({"sso.connector.cloud.available"})` back to `@ConditionalOnProperty({"ssoServiceUrl", "security.oauth2.resource.jwk.key-set-uri"})`.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update configuration conditional properties to ensure the bean loads only when the required URLs are present.
intent_record.what: Replaced the properties specified in the @ConditionalOnProperty annotation with new ones.
intent_record.how: Changes the prerequisites for instantiating the IssuerCheckConfiguration bean to check for specific URL properties instead of a general availability flag.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove unused SSO connector cloud availability property from the environment map.
intent_record.what: Removed a specific property mapping for 'sso.connector.cloud.available'.
intent_record.how: Prevents the injection of the obsolete cloud availability property into the application environment.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove trailing empty lines to clean up code formatting.
intent_record.what: Removed trailing empty lines at the end of the method and class.
intent_record.how: This change has no impact on program behavior as it only modifies whitespace.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove obsolete test property from IssuerCheckConfigurationTest.
intent_record.what: Removed a test property string from the TestPropertySource annotation.
intent_record.how: Prevents the injection of the sso.connector.cloud.available property during the test context initialization.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove obsolete assertion for sso.connector.cloud.available property in test.
intent_record.what: Removed a single JUnit assertion for the 'sso.connector.cloud.available' property.
intent_record.how: The test execution no longer checks for the presence or value of the 'sso.connector.cloud.available' environment property.

grouping_result.intent: Update configuration conditional properties to ensure the bean loads only when the required URLs are present.
Remove obsolete assertion for sso.connector.cloud.available property in test.
Remove trailing empty lines to clean up code formatting.
Remove obsolete test property from IssuerCheckConfigurationTest.

consistency_review.core_intent: Remove unused SSO connector cloud availability property and its associated assertions.

relevance_review.reason: The group updates the configuration conditional properties, which directly addresses the root cause of the incorrect conditional property on the IssuerCheckConfiguration.
The group removes the 'sso.connector.cloud.available' property, which is directly tied to the vulnerability root cause where the IssuerCheckConfiguration was incorrectly conditioned on this property.
The group only contains cosmetic changes (removing trailing empty lines) and does not address the logic related to the IssuerCheckConfiguration or the sso.connector.cloud.available property.

The removal of the obsolete test property from IssuerCheckConfigurationTest directly corresponds to the fix for the vulnerability, which involved correcting the conditional property used for IssuerCheckConfiguration.

candidates.rationale: This annotation incorrectly conditions the loading of IssuerCheckConfiguration on the 'sso.connector.cloud.available' property, which is the root cause of the vulnerability.

vuln_statements.explanation: This annotation incorrectly conditions the IssuerCheckConfiguration on 'sso.connector.cloud.available', preventing issuer validation when not explicitly bound to an SSO service, which is the root cause of the vulnerability.
